_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Inability to lift the tongue to the upper dental alveolus
Impaired side-to-side movement of the tongue
Notched or heart shape to the tongue when it is protruded
Abnormally short frenulum, inserting at or near the tip of the tongue

claim.label: Documentary evidence of failed/ non-indicated conservative management of ankyloglossia in patient aged >/= 2 years

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure?
Did the clinical notes mentioning about grading of ankyloglossia submitted?
On intraoral examination, short lingual frenum and restricted tongue movements were observed?
Is the reason(s) for performing the procedure/ surgery mentioned?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise available at the time of discharge?
Were the pre and post-operative clinical photographs submitted?
Documentary evidence of failed/ non-indicated conservative management of ankyloglossia in patient aged >/= 2 years?
On intraoral examination, short lingual frenum and restricted tongue movements were observed?
Is the reason(s) for performing the procedure/ surgery mentioned?

common_queries: Clinical notes not detailing history, signs & symptoms, or indication for procedure at pre-authorization.
Clinical notes not mentioning grading of ankyloglossia at pre-authorization.
Short lingual frenum and restricted tongue movements not documented on intraoral examination.
Reason(s) for performing the procedure/surgery not mentioned.
Pre and post-operative clinical photographs not submitted at claim stage.
For patients aged >= 2 years, documentary evidence of failed/non-indicated conservative management is missing at claim stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Ankyloglossia, or tongue-tie, is a congenital anomaly in which a short, lingual frenulum or a highly-attached genioglossus muscle restricts tongue movement (ie, restrictive lingual frenulum).
surgical_options: Frenotomy - simple release, or "clipping," of the frenulum. This procedure is often performed for infants with breastfeeding difficulty, with or without local anesthesia.

Frenuloplasty — release of ankyloglossia with plastic repair. It is reserved for ankyloglossia that is not relieved by simple division of the frenulum, posterior tongue-ties, and revision cases. Frenuloplasty requires general anesthesia, sometimes by mask but more commonly with oro- or nasotracheal intubation.
